---
title: "wind_interpolation"
author: "Maya Green"
date: "2026-03-17"
output: html_document
---

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(echo = TRUE)
```

```{r}
library(reticulate)
py_install(c("geopandas", "scipy", "rasterio", "numpy"))
```

```{python}

import geopandas as gpd
import numpy as np
from scipy.interpolate import RBFInterpolator
import rasterio
from rasterio.transform import from_bounds
import os

# Set working directory
working_dir = os.path.expanduser("~/Documents/github/green-bces/processed_evd")
output_dir = os.path.join(working_dir, "processed_evd")
os.makedirs(output_dir, exist_ok=True)

# Load shapefile
gdf = gpd.read_file(os.path.join(working_dir, "Copernicus_era5_summary_wide.shp"))

# Get coordinates
coords = np.array(list(zip(gdf.geometry.x, gdf.geometry.y)))

# Set up output grid
xmin, ymin, xmax, ymax = gdf.total_bounds
cell_size = 350.0
grid_x, grid_y = np.meshgrid(
    np.arange(xmin, xmax, cell_size),
    np.arange(ymin, ymax, cell_size)
)
# grid_points = np.array(list(zip(grid_x.ravel(), grid_y.ravel())))
grid_points = np.column_stack([grid_x.ravel(), grid_y.ravel()])


# Get numeric fields only
numeric_fields = gdf.select_dtypes(include=[np.number]).columns
print(f"Fields to interpolate: {list(numeric_fields)}")

for field in numeric_fields:
    print(f"Interpolating {field}...")
    try:
        values = gdf[field].values
        
        # RBFInterpolator with thin plate spline (closest to regularized spline)
        interpolator = RBFInterpolator(coords, values, 
        smoothing=0.1, 
        kernel='thin_plate_spline' 
      ) 
        grid_values = interpolator(grid_points).reshape(grid_x.shape)
        
        # Save as GeoTIFF
        output_path = os.path.join(output_dir, f"{field}_spline.tif")
        transform = from_bounds(xmin, ymin, xmax, ymax, grid_x.shape[1], grid_x.shape[0])
        with rasterio.open(
            output_path, "w",
            driver="GTiff",
            height=grid_x.shape[0],
            width=grid_x.shape[1],
            count=1,
            dtype=grid_values.dtype,
            crs=gdf.crs,
            transform=transform
        ) as dst:
            dst.write(grid_values, 1)
        print(f"Saved: {output_path}")
    except Exception as e:
        print(f"Error on {field}: {e}")

print("Script complete")
```
```
